episode_summary: Guest QI elf Manu joins Dan, Andy and James for a tour through seaweed science, classic Disney voice acting trivia and wooden architecture, Spain’s subtle tectonic rotation and the story of plate tectonics, and the surprising history of sperm whale oil in cars and other products. It’s a mix of biology, geology, and industrial history with plenty of odd culinary detours.

facts.fact: Okay, today my fact is, seaweed bubbles could stop you getting pregnant.
My fact this week is that in Disney's three little pigs, the pig who built his house out of bricks was voiced by a man who were up in a house made of sticks.
Okay, my fact this week is, Spain is spinning.
My fact is, in the 1970s, the American car industry complained about new rules which said you could no longer include sperm whale brain oil.
